reasoning: The document suffers from severe content loss within critical structured elements, specifically the truncation of the attendance table (missing ~22 rows) and the complete omission of data in the roll call votes table. These defects are corroborated by the programmatic signal flagging fabricated/mismatched tables and an LLM fidelity score of 0.56, which falls into the weak quality band. Despite high shingling recall, the loss of structured data and broken heading hierarchy make this document unfit for automated approval.

concerns.analysis: Two tables in the output are missing a material number of rows. The 'Meeting Attendees January 7, 2021' table in Section 2 (source page 2) contains only 1 data row in the JSON despite the source image showing approximately 23 rows of committee members and staff. Additionally, the 'Roll Call Votes' table in Section 2 (source page 4) is entirely empty of data rows in the JSON, whereas the source document records votes for 11 different members. concerns.output_quote: Section 'Standards Meeting Minutes January', element index 4 (Attendance table) contains only one data row ('Commissioner Marty Cooke'). Element index 9 (Roll Call Votes table) contains no data rows.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: Section 2 ('Standards Meeting Minutes January'), which covers pages 2, 3, and 4 of the source, suffers from a significant omission of content due to the failure to capture the data rows in the attendance and voting tables. This missing data constitutes a large portion (estimated >30%) of the total informational content intended for that specific section, resulting in a substantial loss of fidelity compared to the source.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Section 2 (Pages 2-4) includes attendance and vote records for 23 and 11 individuals respectively, which are integral to the meeting minutes documentation.
concerns.output_quote: The structured JSON for Section 2 omits 22 out of 23 attendance records and all 11 roll call vote records, retaining only high-level headers and point-form minutes.

concerns.analysis: The output document consistently uses Heading 3 (H3) for all major section titles (e.g., 'heading-1', 'heading-2', 'heading-4'). There are no Heading 1 (H1) or Heading 2 (H2) elements present within the sections. This represents a break in the heading hierarchy as it skips the primary and secondary levels required for logical navigation.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The document contains main titles and section headers like 'North Carolina 911 Board Standards Committee Meeting Agenda' and 'Standards Committee Goals'.

concerns.analysis: Two tables in the output are severely truncated. The 'Attendance' table on page 2 of the source contains over 20 entries for committee members and staff, but the output table only contains a single data row (Commissioner Marty Cooke). Similarly, the 'ROLL CALL VOTES' table on page 4 of the source contains 11 rows of voting data, while the output table contains only the header row and zero data rows. This loss of structural data constitutes a major corruption.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2 and Page 4 of the source PDF contain detailed tables for Attendance and Roll Call Votes with multiple rows of data.
concerns.output_quote: The table at sections[1].elements[4] contains only one data row for Marty Cooke. The table at sections[1].elements[7] contains only a header row.

concerns.analysis: Several meaningful logos present in the source document are missing in the HTML conversion. Specifically, the 'NC DIT' logo from page 4 (Roll Call Votes) and the large 'NCDIT | North Carolina Department of Information Technology' logo from the top of page 7 (2022 Standards Committee Dates) are absent from their respective sections in the rendered output.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 7 features a large branding logo for the North Carolina Department of Information Technology at the top of the page. Page 4 also contains the NC DIT 911 Board logo.
concerns.output_quote: The section '2022 Standards Committee Dates' begins directly with text, omitting the logo. The 'Roll Call Votes' section also lacks the logo present in the source.
concerns.location: Section '2022 Standards Committee Dates' (PDF page 7) and 'Roll Call Votes' (PDF page 4).
